hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'gloria' (same form in 1st declension), but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'gloria' (same form), but 'Gloria' is the subject of this exclamation

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'gloriae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'gloriae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'gloriae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'altissimi/altissima' — 'altissimis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.Dative: 'altissimis' could be dative, but after 'in' it is ablative of place
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural would be 'altissimos/altissima' — 'altissimis' is ablative after 'in'

hints.Positive: Positive degree would be 'altis' — the -issim- infix marks the superlative
hints.Comparative: Comparative would be 'altioribus' — the -issim- infix marks the superlative
hints.Diminutive: Diminutive is a noun category, not an adjective degree — 'altissimis' is superlative

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Deo' is dative (glory to God)
hints.Ablative: 'Deo' could be ablative, but here it means 'to God' — dative of reference
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Dei' — 'Deo' is dative meaning 'to God'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Dei' ends in -i

translation: Glory to God in the highest
